---
title: "Curriculum Vitae"
---

*Last updated: February 2026* &nbsp; | &nbsp; [Download PDF](Kim_Inik_cv_26.pdf)

---

## Education

**Ph.D., Psychology** *(2023 – present)*
University of California, Riverside, USA
Advisor: Dr. Weiwei Zhang

**M.A., Psychology** *(2018 – 2020)*
Jeonbuk National University, South Korea
Advisor: Dr. Chang-Ho Park

**B.A., Psychology** *(2011 – 2017)*
Jeonbuk National University, South Korea

---

## Lab Experience

**Cognitive Neuroscience of Perception, Attention, and Memory Lab** *(2023 – present)*
Directed by Dr. Weiwei Zhang
Department of Psychology, University of California, Riverside
[https://memory.ucr.edu/](https://memory.ucr.edu/)

**Affective Neuroscience Lab** *(2021 – 2023)*
Directed by Dr. Jongwan Kim
Department of Psychology, Jeonbuk National University

**Cognition and Perception Lab** *(2018 – 2021)*
Directed by Dr. Changho Park
Department of Psychology, Jeonbuk National University

---

## Publications

**Kim, I.**, Kim, H., Jang, J., & Kim, J. (2022). Measuring consistency of affective responses to ASMR stimuli across individuals using intersubject correlation. *The Korean Journal of Cognitive and Biological Psychology*, 34(2), 121–133.

**Kim, I.**, & Park, C. (2022). The influence of perceptual load on target identification and negative repetition effect in post-cueing forced choice task. *Korean Journal of Cognitive Science*, 33(1), 1–22.

Park, C., **Kim, I.**, & Kim, J. (2023). Affective representations of basic tastes and intensity using multivariate analyses. *The Korean Journal of Emotion & Sensibility*, 26(2), 39–52.

**Kim, I.**, Kim, H., & Kim, J. (2023). Examining the consistency of continuous affect annotations and psychophysiological measures in response to emotional videos. *International Journal of Psychophysiology*, 193, 112242.

**Kim, I.**, & Kim, J. (2024). Affective Representation of Behavioral and Physiological Responses to Emotional Videos using Wearable Devices. *The Korean Journal of Emotion & Sensibility*.

Xie, W., Ma, T., Thakurdesai, S., **Kim, I.**, & Zhang, W. (2024). Discrimination of mnemonic similarity is associated with short-term and long-term memory precision. *Memory & Cognition*, 1–13.

Ma, T., **Kim, I.**, Varastegan, S., Yang, L., Golding, J., & Zhang, W. (2025). Attention is Still a Productive Framework. *Behavioral and Brain Sciences*, 48, e148.

---

## Conference Presentations

Ma, T., **Kim, I.**, Punzalan, D., & Zhang, W. (May 2026). Revisiting evidence against the discrete-capacity account of STM limits. Vision Science Society, St. Pete Beach, FL. *Poster.*

**Kim, I.**, Ma, T., Liu, L., & Zhang, W. (May 2026). Dissociating Strength and Precision in Working Memory Representations. Vision Science Society, St. Pete Beach, FL. *Poster.*

**Kim, I.**, Won, B.Y., Acebo, A., & Zhang, W. (May 2025). Direct Competition Between Hippocampal Pattern Separation and Short-Term Memory Precision. Vision Science Society, St. Pete Beach, FL. *Poster.*

**Kim, I.**, & Zhang, W. (May 2024). Visual working memory for configural information. Vision Science Society, St. Pete Beach, FL. *Poster.*

**Kim, I.**, & Kim, J. (November 2022). Intersubject correlation of physiological responses to discrete emotions. Psychonomics, Boston, MA. *Poster.*

Lee, S., **Kim, I.**, & Kim, J. (April 2022). Comparison between standard deviation and inter-subject correlation as individual consistency measures. Society and Affective Science, Virtual. *Poster.*

**Kim, I.**, Jang, J., & Kim, J. (November 2021). Measuring consistency between individuals of affective responses to ASMR stimuli. International Conference of Korean Emotion and Sensibility Society, Virtual. *Talk.*

Lee, B., Gho, H., Cho, E., **Kim, I.**, Kim, H., & Kim, J. (August 2021). Effects of pleasant and unpleasant ASMR on working memory. Korean General Psychology Society, Virtual. *Poster.*

Choi, Y., Choi, S., Lee, B., **Kim, I.**, & Kim, J. (August 2021). The effect of olfactory stimulation on emotional stability and working memory. Korean General Psychology Society, Virtual. *Poster.*

Gho, H., Choi, E., Kim, H., Jang, J., **Kim, I.**, & Kim, J. (August 2021). The differences in emotional experience by modality of ASMR. Korean General Psychology Society, Virtual. *Poster.*

**Kim, I.**, Ji, S., & Park, C. (August 2021). The effect of auditory ASMR stimuli on the performance in the emotional Stroop task. Korean General Psychology Society, Virtual. *Poster.*

**Kim, I.**, & Park, C. (August 2020). The influence on negative repetition effect of perceptual load. Korean Cognitive and Biological Psychology Society, Virtual. *Poster.*

---

## Awards & Scholarships

**Brain Korea 21 Research Scholarship** *(2022 – 2023)*
Ministry of Education, Science, and Technology

**Oral Presentation Award** *(November 2021)*
International Conference on Emotion and Sensibility 2021

**University Scholarship** *(2011 – 2019)*
Jeonbuk National University

---

## Skills

| Category | Tools |
|---|---|
| Data Analysis | MATLAB, Python, R, SPSS, JASP, Jamovi |
| Statistical Analysis | R, SPSS, JASP |
| Computational Modeling | MATLAB |
| Machine Learning | Python |
| Programming | Python, MATLAB |

---

## References

**Dr. Weiwei Zhang** — Ph.D. Advisor
Department of Psychology, University of California, Riverside
[wwzhang@ucr.edu](mailto:wwzhang@ucr.edu)

**Dr. Jongwan Kim**
Department of Psychology, Jeonbuk National University, South Korea
[jongwankim80@jbnu.ac.kr](mailto:jongwankim80@jbnu.ac.kr)

**Dr. Chang-Ho Park** — M.A. Advisor
Department of Psychology, Jeonbuk National University, South Korea
[finnegan@jbnu.ac.kr](mailto:finnegan@jbnu.ac.kr)
